sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Eastman Chemical (EMN) Boosts Dividend for 16th Consecutive Year
feed.summary: Eastman Chemical (EMN) has increased its quarterly dividend for the 16th consecutive year, raising it from 83 cents to 84 cents per share. This move reflects the company's strong financial health and commitment to shareholder value, with distribution set for January 8, 2026. Despite recent stock price declines, valuation metrics suggest the specialty chemical company may be undervalued, and it maintains efficient operations and a balanced balance sheet.

feed.title: WEC Energy Group (NYSE: WEC) sets next year EPS view at $5.51-$5.61 range guidance
feed.summary: WEC Energy Group (NYSE: WEC) announced plans to increase its quarterly dividend by 6.7% to 95.25 cents per share in Q1 2026, targeting an annual rate of $3.81. The company also provided 2026 earnings guidance in the range of $5.51 to $5.61 per share and reiterated a long-term EPS growth target of 7-8% compounded annually. This dividend hike aligns with its strategy to maintain a dividend payout ratio of 65-70% of earnings.

feed.title: PTC Names Jennifer DiRico as New CFO Effective 2026
feed.summary: PTC Inc. has announced Jennifer DiRico will become the new Executive Vice President and CFO starting January 1, 2026, succeeding Kristian Talvitie. DiRico, currently CFO at Commvault, brings extensive finance and business operations experience to the technology company, known for its CAD and PLM software solutions. This leadership change comes as PTC demonstrates strong financial health with consistent revenue growth, expanding margins, and a favorable valuation.

feed.title: Medtronic (MDT) Maintains Quarterly Dividend of $0.71 Per Share
feed.summary: Medtronic plc (MDT) has maintained its quarterly dividend of $0.71 per share, offering a forward yield of 2.78%. The company demonstrates strong financial health with consistent revenue growth, robust profitability metrics, and a solid balance sheet. While valuation metrics are near historical highs, analyst sentiment remains positive, suggesting market confidence in the medical device giant.

feed.title: Cooper Companies stock soars after strategic review announcement By Investing.com
feed.summary: Cooper Companies Inc. (NASDAQ:COO) saw its stock jump 11.3% after announcing a formal strategic review and reporting better-than-expected fiscal fourth-quarter results. The company's comprehensive review aims to enhance long-term shareholder value through various strategic options. Additionally, Cooper Companies announced a leadership transition and provided positive fiscal 2026 guidance.

feed.title: PTC names Jennifer DiRico as CFO, succeeding Kristian Talvitie
feed.summary: PTC Inc. has appointed Jennifer DiRico as its new Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer, effective January 1, 2026, succeeding Kristian Talvitie. DiRico, formerly CFO at Commvault Systems, will receive an annual salary of $575,000, a target annual bonus of $575,000, and restricted stock units valued at $10.75 million. The company affirmed its previously issued financial guidance, noting that Talvitie's departure is not due to any disagreements regarding operations or accounting practices.

feed.title: Cooper Cos. jumps after starting strategic review, naming new board chair (COO:NASDAQ)
feed.summary: Cooper Cos. (COO) share price surged by 12% in after-hours trading following the announcement of a strategic review of its businesses and the appointment of a new board chair. The company plans a comprehensive review covering its corporate structure, strategy, operations, and capital allocation.

feed.title: IDEX Corporation (NYSE: IEX) appoints Sean Gillen as CFO, HR leader Melissa Flores to resign
feed.summary: IDEX Corporation announced the appointment of Sean M. Gillen as Senior Vice President and Chief Financial Officer, effective January 5, 2026, replacing interim CFO Akhil Mahendra. Gillen, previously CFO at AAR CORP., will receive a substantial compensation package. Additionally, Melissa S. Flores, SVP and Chief Human Resources Officer, will resign on December 19, 2025, to pursue a new opportunity.

feed.title: S&P Global completes $1 billion senior notes offering By Investing.com
feed.summary: S&P Global announced the completion of a private offering of $1 billion in senior notes, comprising $600 million at 4.250% due 2031 and $400 million at 4.800% due 2035. The net proceeds are earmarked for general corporate purposes including acquisitions, debt repayment, and stock repurchases. These notes are backed by Standard & Poor’s Financial Services LLC and were issued under a purchase agreement with Citigroup Global Markets Inc., Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC, and HSBC Securities (USA) Inc. as initial purchasers.

feed.title: Product & Service Launches
feed.summary: Franklin Templeton introduces an XRP ETF, Fidelity rolls out a Roth 401(k) for small businesses, and Capital Group partners with KKR to expand access to private markets in new retirement plan offerings. Vanguard also launches a core-plus bond index ETF providing diverse exposure to the U.S. taxable bond market. These product and service launches aim to offer varied investment solutions across digital assets, retirement savings, and private and public markets.

feed.title: Merck & Co., Inc. (MRK) Stock: Gains FDA Nod for New Cattle Antiparasitic
feed.summary: Merck & Co., Inc. (MRK) has received conditional FDA approval for two new cattle antiparasitic treatments, Exzolt Cattle-CA1 and Dectomax-CA1, aimed at combating New World screwworm larvae and fever ticks. This regulatory milestone addresses urgent animal health needs in the U.S. agriculture sector, providing cattle producers with solutions that could prevent significant economic losses. Despite the approval, Merck's stock experienced a muted reaction and has underperformed major benchmarks across various trailing periods.

feed.title: Equinix CFO to retire in 2026 after 27-year company tenure
feed.summary: Keith Taylor, CFO of Equinix, announced his retirement effective in 2026 after 27 years with the company, including two decades as finance chief. He will remain as a strategic advisor to CEO Adaire Fox-Martin until March 1, 2027, assisting with the transition. His departure comes as Equinix focuses on significant data center expansion to capitalize on the AI boom and navigate past a short-seller report and subsequent SEC investigation that concluded with no enforcement action.

feed.title: Apple shuffles executive ranks again, as Adams and Jackson set to depart (AAPL:NASDAQ)
feed.summary: Apple is undergoing another executive reshuffle, with long-time general counsel Kate Adams and head of environmental policy Lisa Jackson set to leave the company. Jennifer Newstead will take over as the new general counsel, leading both legal and government affairs, while the departure of Jackson raises questions about the future direction of Apple's sustainability initiatives. These leadership changes also include adjustments in AI leadership, potentially impacting Apple's machine learning strategy.

feed.title: IDEX Corporation Appoints Sean Gillen as SVP, Chief Financial Officer
feed.summary: IDEX Corporation has appointed Sean M. Gillen as Senior Vice President and Chief Financial Officer, effective January 5, 2026. Gillen previously served as CFO of AAR CORP. and brings extensive experience in financial performance, strategic growth, and M&A. This announcement also notes the departure of Melissa S. Flores, SVP and Chief Human Resources Officer, effective December 19, 2025.

feed.title: Eastman Chemical raises quarterly dividend to $0.84 per share
feed.summary: Eastman Chemical Company announced its Board of Directors approved a quarterly dividend increase from $0.83 to $0.84 per share, marking the 16th consecutive year of dividend increases. The dividend will be payable on January 8, 2026, to stockholders of record as of December 15, 2025. This increase demonstrates the company's commitment to shareholder value, despite recent third-quarter 2025 earnings falling slightly below analyst expectations with an EPS of $1.14 and revenue of $2.2 billion.

feed.title: Ulta Beauty Boosts Fiscal-Year Guidance as Third-Quarter Sales Climb
feed.summary: Ulta Beauty (ULTA) has raised its fiscal-year guidance following strong third-quarter sales results, driven by growth across all categories and channels. The cosmetics and fragrances retailer now forecasts higher earnings per share for the fiscal year than previously projected.

feed.title: Ecolab increases quarterly dividend by 12%, marking 34th annual rise
feed.summary: Ecolab Inc. announced a 12% increase in its quarterly cash dividend to $0.73 per common share, marking its 34th consecutive annual dividend rate increase. This decision reflects the company's strong business performance and confidence in future growth, as it aims for double-digit adjusted diluted earnings per share growth in 2026. Ecolab has a long history of dividend payments, maintaining an unbroken record for 89 consecutive years.

feed.title: DuPont Announces Applicable Percentage and EBITDA Post-Spin-Off
feed.summary: DuPont de Nemours, Inc. announced the Applicable Percentage and Minimum EBITDA following the Qnity spin-off on December 2, 2025. DuPont's Applicable Percentage is set at 56% with a Minimum EBITDA of $1.4 billion, while Qnity's Applicable Percentage is 44%. This information is based on an SEC filing from December 4, 2025.

feed.title: Costco (Nasdaq: COST) taps ex-U.S. Commerce Secretary Gina Raimondo for board nomination
feed.summary: Costco Wholesale Corporation (Nasdaq: COST) announced the nomination of former U.S. Secretary of Commerce Gina Raimondo to its Board of Directors. Chairman Hamilton James highlighted Raimondo's extensive experience in global business, politics, and international security as valuable additions to the board. Costco, a major retailer, operates 921 warehouses worldwide and maintains e-commerce sites in several markets.

feed.title: Jacqui Krum to join Wynn Macau board, replacing Ellen Whittemore as non-executive director
feed.summary: Jacqui Krum, current Executive Vice President, General Counsel and Secretary of Wynn Resorts, Limited, will join Wynn Macau Ltd's board as a non-executive director starting January 1, 2026. She replaces Ellen Whittemore, who will conclude her tenure on December 31, 2025. This appointment follows Wynn Macau reporting its strongest quarter since COVID in Q3 2025, with revenues reaching US$1.01 billion.

feed.title: Hewlett Packard Enterprise delivers soft revenue outlook after mixed Q4 results
feed.summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (NYSE:HPE) released mixed Q4 results, with revenue missing expectations due to weakness in its server and hybrid cloud businesses, causing its stock to fall over 5% in after-hours trading. Despite a surge in networking revenue following the Juniper Networks acquisition and expanded gross margins, the company issued a soft revenue outlook for Q1 2026. However, HPE did raise its non-GAAP diluted net EPS and free cash flow guidance for the full fiscal year 2026.

feed.title: Old Man Winter Arrives Early: New Jersey American Water Urges Customers to Prepare Pipes and Conserve Water
feed.summary: New Jersey American Water advises customers to take preventative measures against freezing pipes due to unexpectedly early frigid temperatures in the Northeast. The company highlights simple steps such as insulating pipes and testing shut-off valves to avoid costly repairs and offers assistance programs for those worried about high winter bills. Despite the focus on winter preparedness, customers are also reminded that New Jersey is still under a drought watch, emphasizing the ongoing need for water conservation.

feed.title: New Jersey American Water (NYSE: AWK) warns on frozen pipes, drought watch, water use
feed.summary: New Jersey American Water (NYSE: AWK) is urging customers to prepare for an early cold snap to prevent frozen pipes and manage utility bills. The company provided practical tips for pipe protection, safe thawing of frozen pipes, and affordability programs, while also reminding customers about the ongoing drought watch in New Jersey and the importance of water conservation.

feed.title: Canada to Buy Air Strike Weapons from RTX Corp. (RTX) for $2.68 Billion
feed.summary: Canada is set to purchase an air strike weapons system from RTX Corp. (RTX) for $2.68 billion, a move approved by the U.S. State Department. This acquisition is part of Canada's broader initiative to significantly increase its defense spending to meet NATO commitments, aiming for 2% of GDP. RTX stock currently holds a "Moderate Buy" rating from Wall Street analysts, with an average price target indicating a 14% upside.

feed.title: Apple announces departure of general counsel and policy chief
feed.summary: Apple announced the retirement of its general counsel, Kate Adams, and its vice president for environment, policy, and social initiatives, Lisa Jackson. These departures signify a continued trend of senior leadership exits at Apple, following other recent high-profile retirements. Jennifer Newstead, currently Meta's legal and regulatory head, will take over as Apple's new general counsel.

feed.title: Cooper Companies initiates strategic review; shares jump
feed.summary: Cooper Companies (COO) announced it is undertaking a formal strategic review to enhance long-term shareholder value, causing its shares to surge 11.7% in extended trading. This move follows reports that activist investor Jana Partners had built a stake in the company and planned to push for strategic alternatives. The review will focus on improving performance, expanding market share, enhancing operational efficiency, and disciplined capital deployment.

feed.title: Medtronic (NYSE: MDT) declares $0.71 Q3 dividend, 48th year of increases
feed.summary: Medtronic (NYSE: MDT) has announced a cash dividend of $0.71 per share for the third quarter of fiscal year 2026, payable on January 16, 2026, to shareholders of record as of December 26, 2025. This declaration marks the 48th consecutive year of annual dividend increases for the company, affirming its status as an S&P 500 Dividend Aristocrat. The dividend aligns with the increase announced in May 2025, reflecting Medtronic's consistent financial performance and commitment to shareholder returns.

feed.title: Prologis to Announce Fourth Quarter 2025 Results January 21st, 2026
feed.summary: Prologis, Inc. (NYSE: PLD) announced it will host a webcast and conference call on January 21, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. PT/12:00 p.m. ET to discuss its fourth-quarter 2025 results, current market conditions, and future outlook. A live broadcast and replay options will be available for investors and interested parties.

feed.title: American Tower Keeps Quarterly Dividend at $1.70 per Share, Payable Feb. 2, 2026, to Holders of Record Dec. 29
feed.summary: American Tower Corporation announced that it will keep its quarterly dividend at $1.70 per share. The dividend will be payable on February 2, 2026, to shareholders of record as of December 29, 2025. This decision ensures a consistent return for investors in the coming quarter.

feed.title: Wynn Resorts Ltd. stock underperforms Thursday when compared to competitors
feed.summary: Wynn Resorts Ltd. (WYNN) saw its stock price fall by 4.42% to $125.72 on Thursday, underperforming the broader market. This marked the second consecutive day of losses for the company, even as the S&P 500 Index posted a slight gain.

feed.title: PTC (NASDAQ: PTC) appoints Jennifer DiRico CFO, affirms FY 2026 outlook guidance
feed.summary: PTC announced Jennifer DiRico will become Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer, effective January 1, 2026, succeeding Kristian Talvitie. DiRico, previously CFO at Commvault and having played a key role in Toast's IPO and international expansion, will lead PTC's global finance organization. The company also reaffirmed its full fiscal year 2026 and first fiscal quarter guidance as previously announced on November 5, 2025.

feed.title: PTC Announces CFO Transition
feed.summary: PTC (NASDAQ: PTC) announced Jennifer DiRico as its new Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer, effective January 1, 2026. DiRico, currently CFO of Commvault, will succeed Kristian Talvitie, who will serve until December 31, 2025. The company affirmed its fiscal year 2026 and first fiscal quarter guidance.

feed.title: Cooper Companies initiates strategic review
feed.summary: Cooper Companies (COO) has announced that it is beginning a formal strategic review. The purpose of this review is to identify opportunities to enhance long-term shareholder value.

feed.title: Detroit Evening Report: DTE faces protest over push for AI data center
feed.summary: DTE Energy is facing opposition regarding its request to fast-track contracts for a large AI data center project near Saline, Michigan, amid concerns over its substantial energy and water consumption and potential conflict with the state's carbon-neutral goals. Opponents argue the facility would strain resources and that DTE is attempting to circumvent public input. The project's developers maintain the data center operators will cover the energy costs.

feed.title: Fifth Third Bank offers unique customer-friendly account features—but you won’t qualify unless you live in these areas.
feed.summary: Fifth Third Bank is the 18th-largest bank in the U.S., operating over 1,000 branches in 12 states across the East Coast and Midwest. It offers a full range of banking services, including checking and savings accounts, CDs, credit cards, and various loans, with notable customer-friendly overdraft protection policies. However, its savings and CD APYs are often uncompetitive, and its services are exclusively available to residents within its operational footprint.

feed.title: CooperCompanies (Nasdaq: COO) names new chair, prioritizes $2B buyback review
feed.summary: CooperCompanies (Nasdaq: COO) announced the appointment of Colleen Jay as the new Chair of its Board, effective January 2, 2026. Concurrently, the company launched a formal strategic review of its businesses and capital allocation to identify opportunities for simplification and long-term shareholder value creation, including potential partnerships, divestitures, or mergers. During this review period, CooperCompanies plans to focus its capital deployment on repurchasing shares under its recently authorized $2 billion share repurchase program.

feed.title: Take Two Interactive Software Insider Sold Shares Worth $399,953, According to a Recent SEC Filing
feed.summary: An insider at Take-Two Interactive Software recently sold shares valued at $399,953, as detailed in a recent SEC filing. The company (TTWO) is a leading developer and publisher of interactive entertainment, with products sold under brands like Rockstar Games, 2K, and Zynga. Recent news also highlights a further delay for "Grand Theft Auto VI" until November 2026.

feed.title: Levi & Korsinsky Notifies Baxter International, Inc. Investors of a Class Action Lawsuit and Upcoming Deadline – BAX
feed.summary: Levi & Korsinsky, LLP is notifying investors of Baxter International, Inc. (NYSE: BAX) about a class action lawsuit alleging securities fraud between February 23, 2022, and July 30, 2025. The lawsuit claims Baxter made false statements and/or concealed defects in its Novum LVP product, leading to patient harm. Investors have until December 15, 2025, to request appointment as lead plaintiff.

feed.title: VICI Properties (NYSE: VICI) declares $0.45 dividend payable Jan. 8, 2026
feed.summary: VICI Properties Inc. has declared a regular quarterly cash dividend of $0.45 per share for the period from October 1, 2025, to December 31, 2025. This dividend will be payable on January 8, 2026, to stockholders of record as of December 17, 2025. VICI Properties is an S&P 500 experiential real estate investment trust owning a large portfolio of gaming, hospitality, wellness, entertainment, and leisure destinations.

feed.title: Eastman Increases Dividend for 16th Consecutive Year
feed.summary: Eastman Chemical Company has announced an increase in its quarterly cash dividend from $0.83 to $0.84 per share, marking the 16th consecutive year of dividend increases. The dividend is payable on January 8, 2026, to stockholders of record as of December 15, 2025. This action reaffirms the company's commitment to delivering shareholder value and demonstrates confidence in its ability to achieve consistent earnings and strong cash generation.

feed.title: Ecolab Increases Cash Dividend 12%
feed.summary: Ecolab Inc. has announced a 12% increase in its quarterly cash dividend to $0.73 per common share, marking its 34th consecutive annual dividend rate increase. This results in an indicated annual cash dividend of $2.92 per share in 2026. The company attributes this increase to its strong business performance, robust cash flows, and disciplined capital allocation, aiming for continued shareholder value and a target of 12-15% growth in adjusted diluted earnings per share.

feed.title: Intel scraps plan to spin off NEX networking chip business
feed.summary: Intel has decided to retain its multibillion-dollar NEX networking chip business after thoroughly reviewing strategic options, including a potential standalone path. The company believes keeping NEX in-house will enable tighter integration of silicon, software, and systems, strengthening customer offerings across AI, data center, and edge. This decision follows rumors and an earlier confirmation that Intel was considering selling a stake in the unit to outside investors.

feed.title: General Dynamics manager blames crews for Ajax vehicle problems in online post
feed.summary: A manager at General Dynamics Land Systems UK publicly blamed "shite" crews and bad leadership for the persistent problems of excessive noise and vibrations in the Ajax armoured vehicles, which have led to hearing issues for military personnel. This controversial statement follows the re-suspension of the £6.3bn Ajax programme, which faces questions about its future despite earlier assurances of safety. General Dynamics has launched an internal investigation into the manager's comments, affirming they do not reflect company views.

feed.title: Motorola Solutions (NYSE: MSI) CFO to speak at Barclays Global Technology Conference Dec. 11
feed.summary: Jason Winkler, Executive Vice President and CFO of Motorola Solutions (NYSE: MSI), is scheduled to speak at the Barclays Global Technology Conference on December 11, 2025, at 1:20 p.m. PST. A live webcast and replay of his session will be accessible through the company's Investor Relations website. Motorola Solutions, a global leader in public safety and enterprise security, aims to provide insights into its developments and future plans to investors and analysts at this event.

feed.title: Will Tractor Supply’s All-In Gamble on OpenAI Pay Off?
feed.summary: Tractor Supply has decided to partner exclusively with OpenAI for its AI initiatives, integrating the technology into customer-facing assistants and supply chain operations. While this approach offers consistency and deeper collaboration, industry experts question the wisdom of a single-vendor bet in a rapidly evolving AI landscape, suggesting it might limit access to diverse, best-in-class tools. The move aims to leverage AI for improved customer interaction, operational efficiency, and staff support.

feed.title: Blackstone Leads $2 Billion Loan to Ontario Teachers’ Mitratech
feed.summary: Blackstone Inc. and other private credit firms are providing over $2 billion in financing to Mitratech, a software maker. This loan package will help Mitratech refinance an existing bank loan. Antares Capital is also contributing to the financing, which is offered at 4.75 percentage points over the US benchmark.

feed.title: Leidos to continue as program administrator for Hawai'i Energy – Company Announcement
feed.summary: Leidos (NYSE: LDOS) has secured a new three-year, $127 million contract to continue administering the Hawai'i Energy program and the state's Electric Vehicle (EV) Charging Station Rebate program. This partnership aims to help Hawai'i residents and businesses lower energy costs, cut carbon emissions, and enhance energy resiliency. Leidos has managed the program since 2009, contributing to over $7 billion in energy savings statewide.

feed.title: Kroger to increase new store builds in 2026; Q3 e-commerce sales jump 17%
feed.summary: Kroger plans a significant increase in new store builds in 2026 and reported strong Q3 sales, with e-commerce surging by 17%. The company is also searching for a new CEO and expanding its Harris Teeter banner while introducing new Agentic shopping capabilities on its digital platforms.

feed.title: Form 424B2 JPMORGAN CHASE & CO
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase Financial Company LLC has filed a Form 424B2 for Callable Contingent Interest Notes due December 5, 2030, linked to the Nasdaq-100, Russell 2000, and S&P 500 Indices. The notes offer contingent interest payments if each index is above 70% of its Initial Value, with early redemption options. Investors face the risk of principal loss if any index falls below 65% of its Initial Value at maturity.

feed.title: Jakks Pacific Announces a Sweet New Licensing Partnership with The Hershey Company
feed.summary: Jakks Pacific and The Hershey Company have partnered to create a new line of "Charming" small dolls and collectibles, marking Hershey's first confection-themed expansion in this category. The collection will feature dolls inspired by popular Hershey's treats, each with unique charms and accessories, with the first Hershey's Kisses collectibles launching exclusively at CVS on December 26, 2025. This collaboration aims to blend the appeal of beloved treats with self-expression through trendy, collectible mini-dolls.
